page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 virtual CPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor architecture includes a 54 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA-NI.

For storage and networking, the instance includes 7,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, memory read bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. Single-core performance and memory write speeds fall into the average and strong tiers, respectively.

The server's high resource density and local storage capacity make it suitable for workloads requiring high-speed local data access and substantial network throughput. It is designed for database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, 512 GB of DDR4 memory, and 7,600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read bandwidth, alongside strong database and web serving capabilities. Single-core performance is average. This server is designed for high-throughput workloads, including large-scale databases, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing.

tagline: A bare-metal platform combining high-density multi-core compute, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise workloads.

512 GB DDR4 memory and 7,600 GB local NVMe SSD storage
